entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: A lack of sufficient water, described as the most prominent abiotic stress.

entities.name: CROP YIELDS
entities.description: The amount of produce obtained from crops, limited by water deficit.

entities.name: LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The means of securing the necessities of life, reduced by low crop yields.

entities.name: FARMERS
entities.description: People engaged in agriculture, specifically poor farmers in the SAT.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: A geographical region characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures.

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: A family of plants that includes groundnut and chickpea.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop grown in the SAT.

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop grown in the SAT.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of plants to withstand periods of low water availability.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: Traditional methods of crop improvement.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL MECHANISMS
entities.description: The biological processes underlying plant function, specifically related to drought tolerance.

entities.name: POLYMORPHISM
entities.description: Variation in genetic traits, insufficient for drought tolerance breeding.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crop Research Institute for Semi-Arid Tropics.

entities.name: STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of plants to withstand adverse environmental conditions.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC APPROACH
entities.description: Using genetic engineering to introduce genes into plants.

entities.name: MOLECULAR INTROGRESSION
entities.description: The process of introducing genes into a plant's genome.

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: Genetic units responsible for traits, specifically those conferring stress tolerance.

entities.name: TRANSFORMATION PROCESS
entities.description: The method of introducing foreign genetic material into a plant.

entities.name: STRESS CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions that challenge plant growth, used for evaluating transgenic plants.

entities.name: WHOLE PLANT LEVEL
entities.description: Considering the entire plant's response to genetic modifications.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The use of genetic engineering to modify plants.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of product obtained from a crop.

entities.name: TRAIT-BASED APPROACH
entities.description: A strategy for crop improvement focusing on specific traits.

relationships.weight: Low crop yields negatively impact livelihoods.
Legumes are cultivated in the semi-arid tropics.
Groundnut is an example of a legume.
Chickpea is an example of a legume.
Conventional breeding aims to improve drought tolerance.
Conventional breeding is limited by a lack of understanding of physiological mechanisms.
Conventional breeding is limited by insufficient polymorphism.
ICRISAT works to address constraints caused by water limitations.
The transgenic approach aims to improve stress tolerance.
The transgenic approach utilizes molecular introgression.
Successful transformation is required for incorporating stress tolerance.
Transgenic plants are evaluated under stress conditions.
Transgenic technology aims to improve drought tolerance.
Yield is broken down into its components.
